mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window or positive returns occur in fewer than 45% of declining rate periods.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 5.0%, labor market deterioration signals weakening consumer demand and lower corporate earnings, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers positive 60-day returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 5.0%, or the signal loses statistical significance in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in retail sales and housing starts indicate a synchronized contraction in consumer spending and construction activity, which reduces near-term corporate revenue expectations and produces negative equity returns.
failure_condition: The joint decline of RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict negative returns with an accuracy exceeding 50%, or the interaction term loses explanatory power in a multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during periods of stable inflation reflects healthy capacity utilization and supply-side momentum, which supports short-term equity price appreciation over the next week.
failure_condition: Positive 5-day returns do not materialize in more than 40% of rising INDPRO observations within the low inflation regime, or the mechanism reverses direction when CPIAUCSL growth exceeds 3.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Steady growth in total nonfarm payrolls increases household income and consumer spending capacity, which directly boosts corporate earnings forecasts and generates positive S&P 500 returns over the following month.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth fails to correlate with positive 20-day returns, yielding a Rank IC below 0.01 over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation triggers expectations of tighter monetary policy and margin compression, which suppresses equity valuations and produces negative forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: S&P 500 returns remain positive in over 60% of high-inflation trigger months, or the negative correlation vanishes when FEDFUNDS remains unchanged.

mechanism_narrative: Weak real GDP growth combined with rising federal funds rates creates a stagflationary environment that erodes profit margins and increases discount rates, leading to negative equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The combination of low GDPC1 growth and rising FEDFUNDS does not predict negative returns with statistical significance, or positive returns exceed 55% of triggered cases.

mechanism_narrative: Consecutive monthly declines in industrial production signal deteriorating manufacturing output and supply chain constraints, which reduce near-term earnings expectations and drive negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO declines for two consecutive months
failure_condition: Two consecutive INDPRO declines fail to precede negative 20-day returns in more than 50% of historical instances, or the predictive power disappears when UNRATE remains below 4.5%.
